title: Marriage sign and children
summary_text: Hosea's marriage to Gomer becomes a living sign of Israel's unfaithfulness. Their children receive names that announce scattering, no mercy, and a reversal of covenant status. Yet the chapter still promises a future restoration under one head.
key_points: Hosea's marriage to Gomer becomes a living sign of Israel's unfaithfulness.
Their children receive names that announce scattering, no mercy, and a reversal of covenant status.
Yet the chapter still promises a future restoration under one head.

title: Return and renewed betrothal
summary_text: Israel is told to put away her idols and return to her husband the LORD. God will hedge her way, expose her lovers, and speak tenderly again in the wilderness. The chapter moves from discipline to renewed betrothal and covenant joy.
key_points: Israel is told to put away her idols and return to her husband the LORD.
God will hedge her way, expose her lovers, and speak tenderly again in the wilderness.

title: Redeeming Gomer
summary_text: Hosea buys Gomer back and asks her to remain in waiting and sobriety. The sign points to a long season when Israel will live without king, sacrifice, pillar, or ephod. Love preserves the covenant even while discipline continues.
key_points: Hosea buys Gomer back and asks her to remain in waiting and sobriety.

title: No truth in the land
summary_text: The land is charged with truthlessness, murder, adultery, and ignorance of God. Priests and prophets fail together, so the people stumble into ruin. Covenant knowledge has collapsed.

title: Judgment on priests and princes
summary_text: Judgment comes on priests, house of Israel, and house of Judah because they have gone after foreign gods. Their sacrifices cannot hide pride, and the nation finds no help in Assyria or Egypt. The LORD withdraws until they admit their guilt.

title: Shallow repentance
summary_text: Israel speaks of returning, but the loyalty is shallow and temporary. God wants steadfast love, not empty ritual, and he is grieved by Ephraim's faithlessness. The chapter presses for true knowledge of God rather than shallow repentance.
key_points: Israel speaks of returning, but the loyalty is shallow and temporary.

title: An overheated oven
summary_text: When the LORD would heal Israel, sin and conspiracy keep surfacing like an oven overheated by betrayal. The people turn to Egypt and Assyria instead of to God. Their strength melts away because they have sown the wind.

title: The calf of Samaria
summary_text: Israel has broken covenant by making and trusting the calf of Samaria. Their kings and altars will be cut off because they chose what cannot save. The trumpet sound announces that the house of the LORD is rejected.

title: No harvest joy
summary_text: Israel rejoices like a harlot at harvest and then faces exile and barrenness. The land, once a place of covenant blessing, will become unclean and unfruitful. Even the prophet's joy is shadowed by the coming loss.

title: A luxuriant vine
summary_text: Israel is like a vine that produced more altars as God multiplied its fruit. The calves and high places will be destroyed, and the nation will tremble before the king of Assyria. Outward prosperity without loyalty is empty.

title: The child called from Egypt
summary_text: The LORD remembers Ephraim as a child and calls him out of Egypt. Yet the people keep turning away, so judgment and compassion wrestle together within God. He will not abandon them completely because his heart is moved with pity.

title: Wrestling with Jacob
summary_text: Ephraim is condemned for deceit, self-reliance, and rivalry with Jacob's legacy. The prophet recalls Jacob wrestling, weeping, and seeking mercy so that Israel might return. True security comes only by waiting on God.
key_points: Ephraim is condemned for deceit, self-reliance, and rivalry with Jacob's legacy.

title: Death in the grave
summary_text: Ephraim's idolatry leads to death, judgment, and the loss of kingly protection. The LORD says he will be like a lion and a bear to the rebellious. The chapter warns that only God can redeem from the grave.

title: Return and be healed
summary_text: Israel is urged to return, bring words of confession, and abandon the work of idols. God promises to heal backsliding, love them freely, and make them flourish like a green fir tree. The book closes with wisdom for the righteous and a final call to understand these things.
key_points: Israel is urged to return, bring words of confession, and abandon the work of idols.
